
```{r}
check_url_exists <- function(url) {
  tryCatch({
    # Create a request and perform a HEAD pulse
    resp <- httr2::request(url) |>
      httr2::req_method("HEAD") |> 
      httr2::req_perform()
    
    # Status code 200 means it exists and is accessible
    return(httr2::resp_status(resp) == 200)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    # If the URL is broken or the server is down, return FALSE
    return(FALSE)
  })
}
```

# SILVER(WHITING)HAKE

## Map

```{r}
url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/atyrell3/READ-EDAB-stockESP/refs/heads/main/data-raw/2026/SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_map.png"
```

```{r, eval = check_url_exists(url)}
knitr::include_graphics(url)
```

```{r, eval = !check_url_exists(url)}
cat("The map for this species hasn't been created yet.")
```

## Socioeconomic indicators

```{r socioeco-data}
url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/atyrell3/READ-EDAB-stockESP/refs/heads/main/data/intermediate/SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_Commercial_Indicators_Master.csv"
data <- read.csv(url)
```

Get the data used to make the socioeconomic indicator plots [here](`r url`).

### Average price per pound

```{r}
avgprice <- data |>
  dplyr::filter(INDICATOR_NAME == "AVGPRICE_SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_2025_DOLlb") 

NEesp2::plt_indicator(data = avgprice,
                      ar = 1/4,
                      include_trends = TRUE) 
```

### Total commercial landings (pounds)

```{r}
landings <- data |>
  dplyr::filter(INDICATOR_NAME == "Commercial_SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_Landings_LBS") 

NEesp2::plt_indicator(data = landings,
                      ar = 1/4,
                      include_trends = TRUE)
```

### Total commercial revenue

```{r}
totalrev <- data |>
  dplyr::filter(INDICATOR_NAME == "TOTALANNUALREV_SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_2025Dols") 

NEesp2::plt_indicator(data = totalrev,
                      ar = 1/4,
                      include_trends = TRUE)
```

### Number of commercial vessels

```{r}
nvessels <- data |>
  dplyr::filter(INDICATOR_NAME == "N_Commercial_Vessels_Landing_SILVER(WHITING)HAKE") 

NEesp2::plt_indicator(data = nvessels,
                      ar = 1/4,
                      include_trends = TRUE)
```

### Average revenue per vessel ($USD2024)

```{r}
vesselrev <- data |>
  dplyr::filter(INDICATOR_NAME == "AVGVESREVperYr_SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_2025_DOLlb") 

NEesp2::plt_indicator(data = vesselrev,
                      ar = 1/4,
                      include_trends = TRUE)
```

## Ecosystem indicators

### Monthly bottom temperature in stock area

```{r bt-data}
url1 <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/atyrell3/READ-EDAB-stockESP/refs/heads/main/data-raw/2026/SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_hubert_bottomT.csv"
url2 <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/atyrell3/READ-EDAB-stockESP/refs/heads/main/data-raw/2026/SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_glorys_bottomT.csv"
```


```{r, eval = check_url_exists(url1) & check_url_exists(url2), results = "asis"}
cat("Get the data used to make the bottom temperature indicator plots [here (pre-2021)](", url1, ") and [here (2021 and later)](", url2, ").", sep = "")
```

```{r, eval = check_url_exists(url1) & check_url_exists(url2)}
bt_data <- read.csv(url1) |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(read.csv(url2)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(AREA, MONTH, YEAR, INDICATOR_NAME, INDICATOR_UNITS) |>
  dplyr::summarise(DATA_VALUE = mean(DATA_VALUE, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(INDICATOR_NAME = "monthly_bottomT")

for(i in 1:12) {
  print(
    bt_data |>
      dplyr::filter(MONTH == i) |>
      NEesp2::plt_indicator(
        ar = 1/4,
        include_trends = TRUE
      ) +
      ggplot2::ggtitle(paste0("Monthly bottom temperature in stock area - ", month.abb[i]))
  )
}
```

```{r, eval = !(check_url_exists(url1) & check_url_exists(url2))}
cat("Bottom temperature for this species hasn't been calculated yet.")
```

<!-- ### Monthly bottom salinity in stock area -->

<!-- ```{r bs-data} -->
<!-- url3 <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/atyrell3/READ-EDAB-stockESP/refs/heads/main/data-raw/2026/SILVER(WHITING)HAKE_bottomS.csv" -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r, eval = check_url_exists(url3), results = "asis"} -->
<!-- cat("Get the data used to make the bottom salinity indicator plots [here](", url3, ").") -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r, eval = check_url_exists(url3)} -->
<!-- bs_data <- read.csv(url3) |> -->
<!--   dplyr::group_by(AREA, MONTH, YEAR, INDICATOR_NAME, INDICATOR_UNITS) |> -->
<!--   dplyr::summarise(DATA_VALUE = mean(DATA_VALUE, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |> -->
<!--   dplyr::mutate(INDICATOR_NAME = "monthly_bottomS") -->

<!-- for(i in 1:12) { -->
<!--   print( -->
<!--     bt_data |> -->
<!--       dplyr::filter(MONTH == i) |> -->
<!--       NEesp2::plt_indicator( -->
<!--         ar = 1/4, -->
<!--         include_trends = TRUE -->
<!--       ) + -->
<!--       ggplot2::ggtitle(paste0("Monthly bottom salinity in stock area - ", month.abb[i])) -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- } -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r, eval = !(check_url_exists(url3)} -->
<!-- cat("Bottom salinity for this species hasn't been calculated yet.") -->
<!-- ``` -->
